authoritative_sources.title: Modernization of Cosmetics Regulation Act of 2022 (MoCRA)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDA cosmetics-law page defining responsible-person duties, facility registration, product listing, safety substantiation, serious-adverse-event reporting, records access, mandatory recall authority, and GMP rulemaking for cosmetics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulatory baseline and authority hierarchy intake

authoritative_sources.title: Registration and Listing of Cosmetic Product Facilities and Products
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDA workflow reference for Cosmetics Direct, Structured Product Labeling submissions, biennial facility-registration renewals, annual listing updates, and exemption handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Facility registration and product listing review

authoritative_sources.title: FDA Adverse Event Monitoring System (AEMS) Electronic Submissions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDA workflow reference for cosmetic serious-adverse-event electronic submissions, Safety Reporting Portal account setup, attachment handling, and current submission-format expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: Information for Cosmetics Companies
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CDPH guidance for the California Safe Cosmetics Act and the Cosmetic Fragrance and Flavor Ingredient Right to Know Act, including continuous reporting duties, reportable ingredient handling, and use of the state reporting portal.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Jurisdiction-specific ingredient reporting and disclosure handling
used_for: state-specific ingredient and fragrance disclosure boundary

authoritative_sources.title: Draft Guidance for Industry: Cosmetic Good Manufacturing Practices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDA control guidance describing personnel, sanitation, equipment, production, batch, complaint, storage, and records expectations for cosmetic GMP programs.

authoritative_sources.title: FDA Records Access Authority for Cosmetic Products: Guidance for Industry
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDA records-access guidance describing the complaint, safety-substantiation, manufacturing, analytical, distribution, and recall records a cosmetic operator may need to produce.

authoritative_sources.title: Questions and Answers Regarding Mandatory Cosmetics Recalls: Guidance for Industry
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDA workflow guidance for when cosmetic risk handling must escalate into recall strategy, effectiveness checks, communication planning, and regulator-facing recall workflow.

authoritative_sources.title: Cosmetics Labeling Claims
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDA beauty-label guidance on truthful and non-misleading claims and how cosmetic labeling can create drug-treatment issues.

authoritative_sources.title: Is It a Cosmetic, a Drug, or Both? (Or Is It Soap?)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDA intended-use boundary guidance that directly determines when beauty work stays in cosmetic scope and when drug or other adjacent authority is triggered.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Scope determination and adjacent-lane boundary control

authoritative_sources.title: Health Products Compliance Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FTC substantiation guidance describing the evidence discipline needed when beauty claims move into efficacy, treatment, wellness, or health-adjacent statements.

authoritative_sources.title: Endorsements, Influencers, and Reviews
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FTC workflow reference for disclosure, testimonial, material-connection, and review-integrity controls that frequently apply to beauty marketing programs.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 22716:2007 Cosmetics - Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) - Guidelines on Good Manufacturing Practices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 22716 is the recognized cosmetics GMP standard used to normalize manufacturing, storage, shipment, complaints, documentation, and recall-readiness controls across beauty operators.

workflow_stages.name: Regulatory baseline and scope intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify product class, intended use, responsible person, jurisdictions, channels, and the public baseline before any compliance conclusion is produced.

workflow_stages.name: Facility registration, listing, and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map the relevant beauty obligations to registration and listing status, state ingredient-reporting requirements, claims controls, safety-substantiation expectations, GMP controls, and tenant policy overlays for the exact product and market under review.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and systems-of-record review
workflow_stages.description: Validate whether listing, label, claims, batch, supplier, complaint, serious-adverse-event, distribution, and recall artifacts exist in the correct systems of record with traceable ownership.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, remediation, and review preparation
workflow_stages.description: Convert missing evidence, claims conflicts, GMP gaps, complaint issues, and recall dependencies into remediation packets, review-ready summaries, and follow-up owners with explicit blocker logic.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary, recall, and regulator escalation
workflow_stages.description: Refuse legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, toxicology or drug-classification judgments, and final signoff while routing the issue to the correct authorized owner with preserved evidence context.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: Responsible-person designation records, facility registrations, renewals, and product listing submissions or extracts

California Safe Cosmetics reporting records, reportable-ingredient assessments, fragrance and flavor disclosure analyses, and reporting-portal submission confirmations when California sale claims are in scope
Label artwork proofs, ingredient declarations, warnings, claims decks, before-and-after content, influencer briefs, and approval histories
Safety-substantiation dossiers, toxicology summaries, raw-material specifications, supplier questionnaires, certificates of analysis, stability files, and microbiological or analytical results
Batch records, lot genealogy, hold or release status, change-control files, CAPA records, and nonconformance investigations
Complaint logs, intake forms, serious-adverse-event files, submission confirmations, product images, and label copies
Distribution lists, retailer contacts, recall strategy documents, communication trees, effectiveness-check plans, and recall or withdrawal logs
Retailer compliance questionnaires, restricted-ingredient attestations, jurisdiction matrices, and market-entry checklists
Audit or inspection-prep packets, action trackers, management responses, and follow-up verification evidence
systems_of_record: FDA Cosmetics Direct or equivalent registration and listing portal
FDA Safety Reporting Portal or equivalent AEMS submission path

allowed: Map beauty-specific obligations to named controls, evidence artifacts, owners, and review cadence.
Prepare evidence-backed readiness, gap, audit-prep, and remediation summaries for beauty compliance workflows.
Escalate conflicts, missing prerequisites, and adjacent-authority requests without overstating regulator, legal, toxicology, or release authority.
escalate: Missing product class, intended use, responsible person, jurisdiction, facility, or systems-of-record mapping.

Missing California Safe Cosmetics reporting status, reportable-ingredient evidence, fragrance and flavor disclosure context, or other jurisdiction-specific authority artifacts when those markets are claimed.
Conflicting public guidance, retailer requirements, jurisdiction rules, or tenant policy.
Requests that imply legal interpretation, regulator response, toxicology signoff, drug classification, final release, or recall authority.
refuse: Any legal advice, regulator negotiation, or formal legal or regulatory opinion.
Final signoff on formula safety, product classification, launch approval, recall closure, or product release.
Decisions to represent a beauty product as compliant when required listing, labeling, substantiation, complaint, batch, or recall evidence is missing.

domain_failure_modes: Treating beauty compliance as generic GRC work and failing to request product class, responsible person, facility-registration, listing, and claims context.
Marking readiness complete without safety-substantiation, label-control, batch-history, complaint, or serious-adverse-event evidence.
Treating California Safe Cosmetics reporting or fragrance and flavor disclosure duties as covered by the federal baseline and closing readiness without state-reporting evidence.
Letting marketing or retailer deadlines override claims-boundary, labeling, or adverse-event controls.
Failing to distinguish cosmetics from drug-adjacent claims and therefore answering a classification or treatment-question request as if it were in lane.
Ignoring that complaint, batch, distribution, and recall records may live across multiple systems and therefore overstating certainty when the evidence chain is incomplete.
Overgeneralizing from the beauty industry label and answering salon HR, wellness coaching, or unrelated generic compliance work as if it were beauty-compliance work.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Beauty operators may sell products across federal, state, retailer, and international regimes that do not share a single uniform compliance baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: The pack requires runtime retrieval of jurisdiction, channel, retailer overlays, California reporting posture, and market scope before any authoritative mapping or readiness output is produced.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Claims that appear cosmetic in marketing language can shift into drug or health-claim territory depending on intended use, substantiation, and channel context.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: The pack treats classification and legal interpretation as adjacent-lane work and escalates whenever the beauty-versus-drug boundary is not explicit.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: FDA cosmetics GMP implementation continues to rely on published guidance and recognized standards while formal rulemaking posture can evolve.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: The pack uses current FDA GMP guidance plus ISO 22716 as the reviewed public baseline and requires tenant retrieval of stricter or newer jurisdiction-specific controls before asserting a more specific conclusion.
